original_description: Build a standalone, embeddable widget with BundleHive — a React component that ships as a self-contained <script> bundle and mounts onto any website inside an isolated Shadow DOM (Tailwind included). Use this whenever the user wants to create, scaffold, or build an embeddable widget, a third-party embed, a "drop-in <script>" component, a chat/support/booking/feedback launcher, a shadow-DOM web component, a CDN/unpkg widget, or anything a customer pastes onto their own site — even if they don't say "BundleHive" by name. Also use when working inside a BundleHive repo (files named embed.ts, defineWidget, or bundlehive.config), or when adding runtime config, a floating launcher, or a command-queue loader to an embeddable component.
best_description: Build a standalone, embeddable widget with BundleHive — a React component that ships as a self-contained <script> bundle and mounts onto any website inside an isolated Shadow DOM (Tailwind included). Use this whenever the user wants to create, scaffold, or build an embeddable widget, a third-party embed, a "drop-in <script>" component, a chat/support/booking/feedback launcher, a shadow-DOM web component, a CDN/unpkg widget, or anything a customer pastes onto their own site — even if they don't say "BundleHive" by name. Also use when working inside a BundleHive repo (files named embed.ts, defineWidget, or bundlehive.config), or when adding runtime config, a floating launcher, or a command-queue loader to an embeddable component.
final_description: Use this skill when the user is building UI that runs on *other people's* websites — code they hand to customers to drop onto their own pages. Signals: "embed", "embeddable", "drop-in `<script>` tag", CDN/unpkg snippet, config via data attributes or an API key, an Intercom-style loader like `widget('open')`, or a floating chat/support/booking/feedback/newsletter/pricing/reviews launcher a third party pastes onto their site. Also use for any React component mounted in a Shadow DOM to shield it from host-page CSS — including "my Tailwind (v4) renders unstyled inside the shadow root" — and for editing an existing embed project (`embed.ts`, `defineWidget`, `bundlehive.config`, widgets under `examples/`). Applies even without the word "BundleHive." Not for your own app's pages, routes, or dashboards, nor for component libraries published for other devs to import.

history.description: Build a standalone, embeddable widget with BundleHive — a React component that ships as a self-contained <script> bundle and mounts onto any website inside an isolated Shadow DOM (Tailwind included). Use this whenever the user wants to create, scaffold, or build an embeddable widget, a third-party embed, a "drop-in <script>" component, a chat/support/booking/feedback launcher, a shadow-DOM web component, a CDN/unpkg widget, or anything a customer pastes onto their own site — even if they don't say "BundleHive" by name. Also use when working inside a BundleHive repo (files named embed.ts, defineWidget, or bundlehive.config), or when adding runtime config, a floating launcher, or a command-queue loader to an embeddable component.

history.description: Use this skill to build a UI widget that runs on *other people's* websites — a component someone else drops onto their page with a `<script>` tag, embeds via a snippet, or loads from a CDN. Reach for it whenever the goal is a third-party embed: a support/chat bubble, booking or feedback launcher, newsletter signup, pricing table, reviews or comment box that customers paste onto their own sites and configure with data attributes, an API key, or JS commands. Also use for any React component that must render inside a Shadow DOM to isolate it from the host page's CSS (including Tailwind-not-styling-inside-shadow-root problems), or when editing an existing embed project (files like embed.ts, defineWidget, bundlehive.config). Skip it for your own app's pages, routes, or dashboards, and for internal component libraries you publish to npm.

history.description: Use this skill when the user is building — or debugging — a component that gets embedded on *other people's* websites via a `<script>` tag, snippet, or CDN link: a chat/support bubble, booking or feedback launcher, newsletter signup, pricing table, reviews or comment box that end users paste onto their own sites and configure with data attributes, an API key, or JS calls like `widget('open')` (Intercom-style command-queue loaders). Also use for any React component that mounts inside a Shadow DOM to isolate it from a host page's CSS — especially "my Tailwind (v4) renders unstyled inside the shadow root" problems — and for editing an existing embed project (files like `embed.ts`, `defineWidget`, `bundlehive.config`, or widgets under `examples/`). This is BundleHive's job even when the user never names it. Do NOT use it for your own app's pages, routes, or dashboards, or for reusable component libraries published to npm for other developers to import.

Also trigger for any React component that must mount inside a Shadow DOM to isolate it from the host page's CSS — including "my Tailwind (v4) renders unstyled inside the shadow root" — and for editing an existing embed project (files like `embed.ts`, `defineWidget`, `bundlehive.config`, or widgets under `examples/`).
